stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of PM2.5 concentration using an air quality sensor. The measurements have a mean of 159.3 µg/m³ and a standard deviation of 12.9 µg/m³. However, calibration tests reveal that an air quality sensor systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.7% and 10.5%. Estimate the true PM2.5 concentration and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 159.3 µg/m³. After correcting for the 7.6% bias: ≈ 172.4 µg/m³. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.7%-10.5%) and sampling variability: [162.5, 182.7]. Point estimate: 172.4 µg/m³.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
